original_hypothesis.title: Vaults may become irrecoverably stuck when transferring canonical NFTs to contracts that reject ERC721/ERC1155 receipts during onMessageInvocation/onMessageRecalled
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ERC721Vault and ERC1155Vault use safeTransferFrom/safeBatchTransferFrom when releasing canonical NFTs to the recipient or returning them on recall. If the recipient (to or srcOwner) is a contract that does not implement the receiver interfaces correctly, the transfer reverts, causing the entire bridge message execution/recall to revert. This can strand messages in NEW/RETRIABLE and lock assets in the vault indefinitely (since the bridge uses push-style delivery).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a common but practical failure mode for NFT bridges because recipients are often contracts (multisigs, vaults). Even without an attacker, it creates user-funds lock risk; with an attacker, a malicious recipient contract can intentionally brick withdrawals to force operational intervention.

proof_of_signal.lines: onMessageInvocation/onMessageRecalled call _transferTokens which safe-transfers canonical NFTs to arbitrary addresses
proof_of_signal.pattern: push-based safeTransferFrom to untrusted contract recipient; revert blocks message finalization

preconditions: Recipient address is a contract that rejects ERC721/ERC1155 receipt (no onERC721Received/onERC1155Received or returns wrong selector)

initial_code_evidence.suspicious_location: ERC721Vault.sol _transferTokens() canonical path; ERC1155Vault.sol _transferTokens() canonical path
initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: push-based asset delivery to potentially non-compliant contracts
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: reverts prevent message completion and may lock NFTs in the vault
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no alternative pull/claim mechanism; no escrowed claimable mapping

analysis_questions: When a vault transfer reverts, does Bridge mark message RETRIABLE and allow destOwner to set FAILED and recall on source?
Can the system reach an unrecoverable state where neither destination execution nor source recall can succeed?
validation_requirements: Simulate message invocation to a rejecting recipient contract and trace resulting status transitions
Check whether user can always choose a different recipient on retry/recall (likely cannot because message data is fixed)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: User (or attacker-controlled recipient) sets `to` to a contract that rejects NFT reception. Destination processing always reverts when trying to safeTransferFrom, leaving message perpetually failing and canonical NFT stuck in vault.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Loss of access to bridged NFTs (locked in vault)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users bridging NFTs to contract recipients
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Likely requires protocol-level rescue/admin upgrade or special-case handling

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests bridging to non-receiver contracts and analyzing recovery paths

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: on-chain pattern of repeated failures for same msgHash

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
